cit.basis: Sector-based CIT under the Income Tax Law No. 38 of 2018 (as amended): 20% standard/industrial/other companies; 35% banks; 24% financial institutions, insurance and leasing; 24% telecom, electricity, mining and main fuel importers (activity-specific rates fall in a 24%-35% band).
cit.note: Administered by the Income and Sales Tax Department (ISTD). The telecom_energy_mining bucket is a simplification of several activity-specific rates.

national_contribution.basis: National Contribution Tax (Art. 11, Income Tax Law No. 38 of 2018) on taxable income at sector-based rates ranging 1%-7%: 1% other/standard companies, 3% banks, 4% financial institutions/insurance/leasing, up to 7% basic mining. Simplification: the combined telecom_energy_mining bucket uses the 7% mining rate as the conservative upper bound; actual per-activity rates in this bucket range 2%-7%.
national_contribution.note: Levied on top of CIT on the same taxable-income base.

vat.note: General Sales Tax (GST) at a standard 16% rate; reduced and zero rates apply to specific goods and services.

personal_income_tax.note: Progressive PIT 5%-30% under Law 38/2018, plus a 1% national contribution on annual taxable income above JOD 200,000.

social_security.note: Social Security Corporation (SSC) contributions: 7.5% employee, 14.25% employer.

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
Sector classification, activity-specific rates, GST regimes, relief, filing, and free-zone/development-zone rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official ISTD guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
